---
title: "testing_document"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(svglite)
library(r2d3)
library(tibble)
library(jsonlite)

```

```{r}
data = tibble(x = seq(1, 25, .5), 
              y = exp((x-15)/30),
              ypoints = exp(((x-15)/30) + rnorm(30, 0, 0.1)))
data$Group <- sample(c("Group_1", "Group_2", "Group_3"),
                     size = nrow(data),
                     replace = TRUE)
```
```{r}
ggplot(data, aes(x, ypoints)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour = "magenta") +
  labs(x = "X-Label", y = "Y-Label")
```

```{r}
(ggplot(data, aes(x, ypoints)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour = "magenta") +
  labs(x = "X-Label", y = "Y-Label")) |> 
  youdrawit_scatter()
```
```{r}
ui <- fluidPage(
  (ggplot(data, aes(x, ypoints)) +
  geom_point(size = 5, colour = "blue", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "X-Label", y = "Hello") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")) |> 
  youdrawit_scatter()
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {

  observeEvent(input$my_shiny_app, {
    cat("Received from JS:\n")
    str(input$my_shiny_app)
  })

}
# Can we now take that shiny app and save it as an R object you can take from a Java to a tibble
# In the example shiny app just to show that it is, render a table 
# Might need to include stuff in the documentation
shinyApp(ui, server)
```
